titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the J. L. Ferrell Papers
titlestmt.author: Michelle Peralta, Abdeena Barrie, and Michaela Gibbons

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday December 12, 2024 at 23:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The J. L. Ferrell papers provide an intimate look at African American life in the South during the later years of the Great Depression into the beginning of World War II. The collection is primarily comprised of correspondence to Joe Louis Ferrell (1915-1986) from his wife Hazel E. Pinkard (1913-1994), family members, and friends. The collection also contains materials related to Ferrell's employement as a driver including vehicle operator licenses, employment identification cards, and receipts for vehicle-related expenses. Ferrell's earning statements, layoff notices, and gasoline ration applications detail the economic reality of the 1930s and 1940s. Various papers relating to Ferrell's military service are also included. Hazel Pinkard Ferrell's letters primarily illustrate her academic and social life while attending Tilloson College, a historic Black college located in Austin, Texas.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The J. L. Ferrell papers provide an intimate look at African American life in the South during the later years of the Great Depression into the beginning of World War II. The collection is primarily comprised of correspondence to J. L. (Joe Louis) Ferrell (1915-1986) from his future wife Hazel E. Pinkard (1913-1994), family members, and friends. The collection also contains materials related to Ferrell's employement as a driver including vehicle operator licenses, employment identification cards, and receipts for vehicle-related expenses. Ferrell's earning statements, layoff notices, and gasoline ration applications detail the economic reality of the 1930s and 1940s. Various papers relating to Ferrell's military service are also included.
Hazel Pinkard Ferrell's letters primarily illustrate her academic and social life while attending Tilloson College, a historic Black college located in Austin, Texas.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are organized by material type.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from James Arsenault & Company on the Arthur Corbitt Hoskins Memorial Fund, 2022.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: J. L. Ferrell Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Archival Description Unit Processing Manual.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: J. L. (Joe Louis) Elmer Ferrell was born January 23, 1915 in Coolidge Texas, one of the at least nine children of Tom Ferrell (1876-1959) and Pearl Briscoe Ferrell (1883-1947). Ferrell worked various jobs as a driver and construction worker around Texas and Oklahoma before serving in the United States Navy during World War II. By 1943, Ferrell was working at Camp Mexia, the largest of Texas' prisoner of war camps that predominately held German Nazis. Ferrell married Hazel Pinkard (born on September 5, 1913) who wrote Ferrell letters detailing her social and academic life while she attended Tillotson College, a historic Black College in Austin, Texas. They married in 1939.
J. L. Ferrell died on April 15, 1986. Hazel Pinkard Ferrell died in June 1994.

did.unittitle: Notice of assessment, Riddle Burial Association

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 1, Folder 32: In-process material pending conservation treatment. For further information, consult Access Services.

did.unittitle: United States Employment Services identification card
Renewal of Highway and Nonhighway Gasoline Rations application form (blank)
